prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='CWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.694) and high seasonality variance (0.8324), with models capturing seasonal patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, FT_rf), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while accounting for their different seasonal capture strategies.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_catboost", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "THETA", "FT_svr", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.694,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.694) and high seasonality variance (0.8324), with models capturing seasonal patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, FT_rf), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while accounting for their different seasonal capture strategies."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": true,
  "debate_margin": 0.03,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon-specific patterns favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.694 and relative_spread_mean=0.224 show high model disagreement, justifying dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst) to leverage diversity while handling phase shifts. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 captures early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf), inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 provides robust weighting with shrinkage=0.25 for stability. Debate forced due to competitive candidates within small margin."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No homogeneity issue (max count=2 < 4 threshold). All candidates are valid from current_candidate_names. Universe leaderboards show better RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE candidates but they are not needed since we already have diverse types and the current set is well-balanced. No leakage detected - inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage design (fits on past windows only), baselines never leak, selection candidates use validation-based selection from past data.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l2100_topk5"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Universe leaderboards show ridge_stacking_l2100_topk5 as top performer on SMAPE (0.1545) and MAPE (0.1504), significantly better than current candidates (~0.176-0.191). Current set lacks any ridge stacking methods, adding it improves diversity. n_windows=3 is low but universe evidence shows ridge_stacking performs well. relative_spread_mean=0.224 (<0.25) so DBA not strongly favored, but current set already has dba_combination.", "changes": ["added ridge_stacking_l2100_topk5 for SMAPE/MAPE improvement"], "when_good": "When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding more ridge_stacking variants and exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0 (best RMSE in universe)"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.694) and high seasonality variance (0.8324), with models capturing seasonal patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost, FT_rf), indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while accounting for their different seasonal capture strategies.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon-specific patterns favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.694 and relative_spread_mean=0.224 show high model disagreement, justifying dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst) to leverage diversity while handling phase shifts. topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 captures early horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf), inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 provides robust weighting with shrinkage=0.25 for stability. Debate forced due to competitive candidates within small margin.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No homogeneity issue (max count=2 < 4 threshold). All candidates are valid from current_candidate_names. Universe leaderboards show better RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE candidates but they are not needed since we already have diverse types and the current set is well-balanced. No leakage detected - inverse_rmse_weights uses anti-leakage design (fits on past windows only), baselines never leak, selection candidates use validation-based selection from past data.

statistician.rationale: Universe leaderboards show ridge_stacking_l2100_topk5 as top performer on SMAPE (0.1545) and MAPE (0.1504), significantly better than current candidates (~0.176-0.191). Current set lacks any ridge stacking methods, adding it improves diversity. n_windows=3 is low but universe evidence shows ridge_stacking performs well. relative_spread_mean=0.224 (<0.25) so DBA not strongly favored, but current set already has dba_combination.
statistician.when_good: When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding more ridge_stacking variants and exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0 (best RMSE in universe)
